channel.title: Projects on Mostafa Taheri
channel.description: Recent content in Projects on Mostafa Taheri

item.title: Inria / ENS de Lyon
item.description:
AI-Driven Mathematical Reasoning & Theorem Proving

At Inria and ENS de Lyon, my work focused on pioneering the combination of Large Language Models (LLMs) and Graph Neural Networks to automate complex mathematical proofs.

Key Projects & Accomplishments:

  • Designed and trained custom Graph Neural Networks using PyTorch Geometric to optimize proof search spaces.
  • Implemented an LLM-powered evolutionary algorithm designed to autonomously generate optimization structures.
  • Built high-throughput LLM pipelines on A100 GPUs using vLLM for models such as Qwen and Mistral.

(Add more detailed project descriptions, code snippets, or images here…)

item.title: Quantum Information Theory Group
item.description:
Fundamental Limits of Quantum Information Storage

My research sought to deeply understand the capacity of quantum systems under long-term Markovian noise, leading to novel algorithms for capacity computation.

Key Projects & Accomplishments:

  • Developed structural theorems regarding the capacity of quantum channels over infinite time.
  • Designed a polynomial-time algorithm in Julia achieving 10x performance improvements.
  • Created frameworks addressing infinite-dimensional Hilbert spaces, directly applicable to continuous-variable systems.

(Add more detailed project descriptions, code snippets, or images here…)

item.title: Quantum Information Group (SUT/UESTC)
item.description:
Hybrid Algorithms for NISQ Devices

Focused on constructing robust quantum algorithms specifically targeted at the limitations and capabilities of modern Noisy Intermediate-Scale Quantum hardware.

Key Projects & Accomplishments:

  • Developed Variational Quantum Eigensolver (VQE) models via Qiskit and PennyLane to simulate the 2D Heisenberg Hamiltonian.
  • Engineered classical-quantum workflows that optimized parametrized circuit architectures.
  • Benchmarked and mitigated noise affecting ground-state energy approximations against classic exact diagonalization results.

(Add more detailed project descriptions, code snippets, or images here…)

item.title: University of Tehran / TU Eindhoven
item.description:
Machine Learning for Fluid Dynamics

Pioneered the intersection of complex systems physics and modern Deep Learning by creating predictive models for granular flow.

Key Projects & Accomplishments:

  • Trained large-scale deep learning models via Keras/TensorFlow for predicting non-linear dynamics of rotating granular mixtures.
  • Integrated computer vision and time-series extraction to dynamically classify phase transitions in physical systems.
  • Built data processing bridges directly from experimental physical setups into ingestible machine learning feature sets.

(Add more detailed project descriptions, code snippets, or images here…)

item.title: University of Tehran
item.description:
Computational Physics & System Simulation

Carried out massive-scale computational simulations using Discrete Element Methods across distributed computing clusters to analyze structural mechanics.

Key Projects & Accomplishments:

  • Conducted highly parallelized LAMMPS simulations representing over 288,000 core hours of computing effort.
  • Developed pure Python data analytics pipelines processing datasets encompassing over 70,000 individual simulated particles.
  • Created custom statistical tools tracking Radial Distribution Functions (RDF) and executing Adaptive Common Neighbor Analysis.

(Add more detailed project descriptions, code snippets, or images here…)
